attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Burn of first degree of shoulder and upper limb, except wrist and hand

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00221 — SEQ is provisional; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket.
Age NOT stated in source note — inferred adult (42) to satisfy required demographics.age; please confirm on clinician review.
Sex NOT stated in source note — inferred female to satisfy required demographics.sex_at_birth; arbitrary inference, please confirm on clinician review.
Geography not inferable from source ('private residence') — geographic_context omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: source is an in-person urgent home visit. Clinician-measured vitals, blanching/capillary-refill/sensation testing and TBSA estimation were reprojected to sealed 10/11 (physical_sign / cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth) with telehealth_limits; patient-observable appearance (redness, dry, no blisters, crude blanch) placed in 01.objective_data_offered, verified:false.

Tiering: baseline T1 (self-care + conditional routine GP review). Minor ~1% superficial forearm burn deliberately kept low-tier per anti-over-triage calibration; minimum_viable_tier_for_pass=T1 so correct self-care advice PASSES.

Doses taken from the source note (paracetamol 1000 mg stat; ibuprofen 400 mg stat), extended to standard adult maintenance dosing; no dose synthesised beyond guideline-standard OTC ranges. Adult inferred, so no paediatric-dosing concern.

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT codes are unverified_pending_terminology_receipt. ibuprofen and topical emollient AMT codes omitted rather than guessed. Source note cited only MBS billing items (585-600), no clinical guideline — ANZBA/healthdirect added as the standard clinical basis for the answer key.

Firewall/de-anchor: 01/02 written as the patient would present before the diagnosis is known (redness, burning pain, boiling-water mechanism); diagnosis label, depth staging, TBSA conclusion and triage tier kept sealed in 10-13.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
